```json
  "findings": [
    "PVC flue pipe is back-pitched, causing condensate to collect in the vent instead of draining back to the furnace or drain point",
    "Back-pitched condensing flue can block exhaust flow, spill flue gases including CO into the living space, and corrode the vent material",
    "Condition violates IRC M1801 and NFPA 54 requirements for proper condensate drainage and vent slope"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop furnace operation immediately and tag out the appliance until venting is corrected",
    "Re-pitch PVC flue to drain condensate back toward the furnace/induced-draft blower or to an approved condensate drain",
    "Verify minimum required slope per OEM installation instructions and IRC/NFPA 54",
    "Inspect and clean any condensate that has collected in the vent or appliance heat exchanger",
    "Test combustion and verify proper draft and CO levels after correction"
  ],
  "rationale": "A back-pitched PVC flue on a condensing furnace is a high-severity safety hazard because it can trap condensate, restrict exhaust flow, cause flue gas spillage/carbon monoxide exposure, and accelerate corrosion. The installation is defective and non-compliant, so the work fails inspection and must be corrected before returning the appliance to service.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801 - Vent systems",
    "NFPA 54 - National Fuel Gas Code"
  ]
}
```
